View this page

app.shared.routes¶

The main routes that handle the API requests regarding starting and monitoring tasks.

app.shared.routes.cancel_task(tracking_id: str) → dict¶

Cancel a task

Parameters:

tracking_id – The ID of the task to cancel

Returns:

A dictionary containing the tracking_id

app.shared.routes.clear_queues(broker: Broker, queue_name: str | None = None) → dict¶

Clear all tasks from queues

Parameters:

  • broker – The broker to clear queues from

  • queue_name – Optional specific queue name to clear

Returns:

Dictionary with clearing results

app.shared.routes.error_wrapper(func)¶

A decorator that catches exceptions and returns them as JSON Flask Response

app.shared.routes.monitor(results_dir: str, broker: Broker) → dict¶

Monitor the app service

Parameters:

  • results_dir – The directory where the results are stored

  • broker – The broker to get the queues from

Returns:

A dictionary containing the total size of the results directory and the queue sizes

app.shared.routes.qsizes(broker: Broker, queue_name: str | None = None) → dict¶

List all tasks in queues with their details

Parameters:

  • broker – The broker to get the tasks from

  • queue_name – Optional specific queue name to list tasks from

Returns:

Dictionary containing tasks per queue or error message

app.shared.routes.receive_task(req: Request, save_dataset: bool = True, use_crops: bool = True) → Tuple[str, str, Dataset | None, dict]¶

Extracts the parameters from the request and returns them

Expected request format:

{
    "experiment_id": "experiment_id",
    "notify_url": "http://example.com/callback",
    "documents": "[
        {"type": "iiif", "src": "https://eida.obspm.fr/eida/iiif/auto/wit3_man186_anno181/manifest.json"},
        {"type": "iiif", "src": "https://eida.obspm.fr/eida/iiif/auto/wit87_img87_anno87/manifest.json"},
        {"type": "iiif", "src": "https://eida.obspm.fr/eida/iiif/auto/wit2_img2_anno2/manifest.json"}
        {"type": "url_list", "src": "https://example.com/urls_list"},
        {"type": "zip", "src": "https://example.com/zipfile.zip"},
    ]",
    "crops": [ # optional
        {
            "doc_uid": "wit3_man186_anno181",
            "source": "imagename.jpg",
            "crops": [
                {
                    "crop_id": "crop_id",
                    "relative": [x, y, w, h]
                }, ...
            ]
        }, ...
    ],
    "parameters": {
        "model": "model.pt"
        ...
    },
}
Parameters:

  • req – The Flask request object

  • save_dataset – Whether to save the dataset to disk

  • use_crops – Whether to use crops

Returns:

The experiment_id, notify_url, tracking_url, and the parameters

app.shared.routes.result(filename: str, results_dir: str, xaccel_prefix: str, extension: str = 'zip')¶

Get the result of a task

Parameters:

  • filename – The ID of the task / the reference of the doc / etc.

  • results_dir – The directory where the results are stored

  • xaccel_prefix – The prefix for the X-Accel-Redirect header

  • extension – The extension of the result file (without the dot)

Returns:

The result file as a Flask response

app.shared.routes.start_task(task_fct: Actor, experiment_id: str, task_kwargs: dict) → dict¶

Start a new task

Parameters:

  • task_fct – The task function to run

  • experiment_id – The ID of the experiment

  • task_kwargs – The parameters of the task

Returns:

A dictionary containing the tracking_id and the experiment_id

app.shared.routes.status(tracking_id: str, task_fct: Actor) → dict¶

Get the status of a task

Parameters:

  • tracking_id – The ID of the task

  • task_fct – The task function

Returns:

A dictionary containing the tracking_id and the log of the task
